item-infusedmash-lilyofthevalley: Infused Lily of the Valley Mash
uppsalchemyguide-title: Uppsala Alchemy: Potions for a better world
uppsalchemyguide-text:
This mod leans heavily on the excellent work found in A Culinary Artillery. Potion making requires Cauldrons, Mixing Bowls, and Bottles from that mod. Specific potions can be excluded from gameplay with the Uppsalchemy config file. Disabling a potion removes all tiers of that potion from the game.

Syntax
These are the common names used throughout the Uppsalchemy Mod
Potion Base - Used for all potions, brewed in a Cauldron and cured by aging.
Aged Potion Base - A critical ingredient in potions and infused mashes made by curing Potion Base.
Unfinished Potion - An intermediate mixture brewed in a Cauldron that can be infused or fixed.
Infused Mash - A reagent used to increase the potency of a potion, formed in a Mixing Bowl.
Tincture - A fixing agent formed in a Mixing Bowl and used to finalize an intermediate potion into a usable form.
Finished Potion - A ready to use potion of at least 0.25 L (one use)
The Potion Pipeline
The potion brewing process begins by creating Potion Base, then allowing the base to cure into Aged Potion Base.
The next step is to brew an intermediate (unfinished potion) by combining Aged Potion Base with specific Flowers. The result is a Tier 1 unfinished potion. Each potion has three tiers of potency. Tier 1 potions have the lowest potency and have the shortest duration.
At this point, there are two options: Finish the potion with a fixing tincture or increase its potency to the next tier with an infused mash.

Here are the full processes for Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 basic potions:
Potion Base: Honey + Dwarf Furze
Aged Potion Base: Potion Base + Time
Tier 1 Potion: Aged Potion Base + Flowers > Tier 1 Intermediate + Tincture > Finished Potion
Tier 2 Potion: Tier 1 Intermediate + Infused Mash > Tier 2 Intermediate + Tincture > Finished Potion
Tier 3 Potion: Tier 2 Intermediate + Time > Cured Tier 2 Intermediate + Infused Mash > Tier 3 Intermediate + Tincture > Finished Potion

There are Advanced Potions that combine the effects of two or three potions.

Tier 1 Advanced Potion:
  Tier 3 Intermediate + Tier 3 Intermediate > Tier 1 Advanced Intermediate
  Tier 1 Advanced Intermediate + Tincture from each basic potion used > Finished Advanced Potion

Tier 2 Advanced Potion:
  Tier 1 Advanced Intermediate + Infused Mash from each basic potion used > Tier 2 Advanced Intermediate
  Tier 2 Advanced Intermediate + Tincture from each basic potion used > Finished Advanced Potion

Tier 3 Advanced Potion:
  Tier 2 Advanced Intermediate + Time > Cured Tier 2 Advanced Intermediate
  Cured Tier 2 Advanced Intermediate + Infused Mash from each basic potion used > Tier 3 Advanced Intermediate
  Tier 3 Advanced Intermediate + Tincture from each basic potion used > Finished Potion

Infused Mash
Higher Tier potions are brewed with a reagent made by infusing Aged Potion Base into the flower used to craft the tier 1 version of the potion. This process uses the Mixing Bowl.
Infused Mash: Aged Potion Base + Flowers

Tincture
Tinctures are fixing agents that convert unfinished potions into their final useable form. Tinctures are made in a Mixing Bowl by combining Seed Oil and the required agent (often crushed ore).
Tincture: Seed Oil + Crushed Ore / Nugget

item-handbooktext-potionportion-base: A raw ingredient for hopeful potion brewers.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-base-age: The foundational component for all potions.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-swiftnes: Grants a brief window of accelerated speed. Higher tiers give faster speeds and longer durations.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-sustenan: Grants a short respite from the nutritional demands of mortal flesh. Higher tiers increase the duration and potency of the respite.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-regenera: Briefly increases the natural mending processes of the body. Higher tiers increase the speed of recovery and duration of effect.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-aqualung: Grants a reprieve from the constant necessity of breathing. Higher tiers increase the duration of this vacation.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-warmth: Grants a stay against the harsh reality of a properly regulated body temperature. Higher tiers extend this protection.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-healing: Grants an immediate return to a time when hitpoints were closer to their maximum. Higher tiers grant a higher return.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-radiance: Shoves light out of every orafice for a time. Higher tiers shove harder and longer.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-featherf: Gives the middle finger to hard landings for a time. Higher tiers keep the finger extended longer.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-vitality: Grants a temporary boost to maximum health. Higher tiers grant a larger boost for a longer time.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-durabili: Grants a short window in which the slings and arrows of outrageous fortune will have little effect. Higher tiers increase the duration and resistance.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-encumbra: Grants a few moments where the weight of the world no longer presses down upon your shoulders. Higher tiers increase the duration and reduce the stomach growls caused by carrying the team.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-jumping: Grants a bouncy window of time where stairs are a suggestion and two meter walls area joke. Higher tiers increase the duration and height.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-strength: Fills you with just enough nostolgia to make everything hit a little harder for a while. Higher tiers hit harder for longer.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-piercing: Fills slings and arrows with sharper and more painful barbs for a short time. Higher tiers increase the shooting pain and duration.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-accuracy: This potion does not aim hack. It just helps untalented archers win medival faire tourneys. Higher tiers help bigger losers longer.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-flow: Grants a brief window of time in which an archer can really empty a quiver. Higher tiers increase the duration and speed.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-drawing: Makes arrows want to leave your presence in a hurry. Higher tiers increase their haste to leave and the duration of the effect.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-orefortu: Increases the luck of the miner for a bit. Higher tiers increase effect and duration.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-huntersf: Increases the skill of a hunter skinning their kill for a bit. Higher tiers increase effect and duration.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-foragers: Increases the yield of a forager grubbing for berries and seeds in the wild. Higher tiers increase the pull of their grubby fingers and the duration of the effect.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-harvestf: Increase the skill of the field worker in bringing in as much flax as possible (and other stuff). Higher tiers means more flax and a longer duration for the potion.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-ancientf: Increases the luck of finding more trash left behind by those that came before. Higher tiers increases the amount of trash and duration of the effect.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-glowingb: An advanced potion that combines the Potion of Radiance with the Potion of Aqualung.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-graceful: An advanced potion that combines the Potion of Jumping with the Potion of Featherfall.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-greaterr: An advanced potion that combines the Potion of Healing and the Potion of Vitality.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-sheltere: An advanced potion that combines the Potion of Warmth and the Potion of Aqualung.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-limitles: An very advanced potion that combines three potions: Radiance, Warmth, and Aqualung.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-suddenre: An advanced potion that combines the Potion of Healing with the Potion of Regeneration.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-expired: Drinking this is an extrodinarily bad idea.
item-handbooktext-tinctureof-*: A tincture used to fix a potion into a drinkable form.
item-handbooktext-infusedmash-*: An infused mash of flowers used to improve an unfinished potion.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-cruelpat: Focuses the mind and power of the archer into a deadly force at the cost of speed.
item-handbooktext-potionportion-welcomep: Focuses the body of a warrior into a vessel of absorption.
